desc: A real stunner, dark winged and iridescent that is mostly green but sometimes appears blue.

fm.Male: iridescent green body and smokey black wings.
fm.Female: Blackish iridescent body, wings black with a white stigma.
fm.Similar Species: River Jewelwing has partially dark wings.
fm.Habitat: Slow woodland streams with brushy vegetation.

desc: Males pruinecent, females brownish and variable. Males of this species are very common and unafraid to get close to observers.  Sometimes they show a broad humeral stripe and othertimes they are all powdery.

fm.Male: Heavy pruinescense is diagnostic within Dancer group
fm.Female: Eyes and body brownish, pattern on top of S9 separates from Blue-fronted.
fm.Similar Species: Female very similar to Blue-fronted. Limited Median and humeral stripe.
fm.Habitat: Wide variety of moving and still waterways.

desc: A small, green-eyed damselfly with distinctive exclamation mark ante-humeral mark. Males can show blue, green or yellow-green throax and narrow abdomen. Females can be bruinescent and dark ante-humeral mark.

fm.Male: Light green, abdomen black dorsally, antehumeral stripe interrupted like an exclamation mark!
fm.Female: Can be pruinescent, interrupted antehumeral stripe. Eyes greenish below.
fm.Habitat: Likes to fly withing vegetation along ponds and still water.

desc: Forktails are small green-eyed dragonflies, slow moving often flying only a few inches when disturbed.  They fly close to grass and often between blades. If not overlooked, easy to observe.

fm.Male: Eyes and face green, thorax green and black, abdomen tipped blue
fm.Female: Immatures are orange, mature eyes and face green, body pruinecent but median and humeral stripes broad.

desc: Most likley to be seen away from water. It is similar to Powdered Dancer but lacks pruinescents, but has a deeply blue thorax sometimes with light tan below.  Face mostly blue.

fm.Male: Blue face and eyes, thorax blue above tan below, abdomen mostly black with blue end.
fm.Female: Variable often brownish, eyes brown with very narrow median and humeral stripe (sometimes lacking).
fm.Similar Species: Powdered Dancer and some females of other species.

desc: Our only violet colored Dancer. This one is such a pleasure to see, just drink in those colors.

fm.Male: Eyes brown, thorax and abdomen with violet, abdomen tip more blue than violet.
fm.Female: Eyes brown, humeral strip may be forked, black down the back of abdomen to S8.
fm.Habitat: Small Streams, ditch water and open spaces.

desc: Common to see in small numbers on large streams or a river. This one has a mostly dark abdomen and very narrow humeral stripe as well as reduced blue on S9.

fm.Male: Blue-green thorax, abdomen mostly black above. Eyes blue, black above with blue dumbbell on top.
fm.Female: Either blue or green thorax. Abdomen black above greenish/bluish below with S9 and 10 blue.
fm.Habitat: Medium streams and rivers, can tolerate poorer water quality.

desc: Males very dark with all dark eyes, females brownish.

fm.Male: Dark, eyes deep blue, post ocular spots separate. Thorax becoming gray with pruinecense otherwise showing split humeral stripe. Abdomen with narrow blue rings.
fm.Female: Eyes brown below. Thorax mostly brown with median stripe and split humeral stripe.
fm.Habitat: Small streams with sandy or rocky banks.

desc: A long bodied blue and yellow spreadwing with pale outer vein.

fm.Male: Eyes blue, thorax yellow with blue antehumeral stripe, broad black median. No pruinosity on abdomen.
fm.Female: Shorter than male, otherwise similar coloring and pattern.
fm.Habitat: Lakes and Ponds, slow moving or still water.

desc: Our only truly orange Bluet but beware some immatures of other species may have orange appearance.

fm.Male: Orange eyes, thorax and abdomen tip distinctive.
fm.Female: Can be tan, pale orange or bluish. Limited pale on S9 and 10. Antehumeral stripe wider than median. Good luck!
fm.Habitat: Still water and small streams, ok with some water pollution.

desc: The bluet with the most blue on the abdomen. Tipically males have small separated posocular spots.  Females wildly polymorphic and can be tan, brown, blue dark blue or even greenish.

fm.Male: Abdomen has broad blue patches on all of the middle segments. Median stripe on thorax widest, humeral stripe can be narrow but middle sized compared to Stream and Azure.
fm.Female: Females tan or bluish usually with tan or brown eyes, sometimes green eyes. May be indistinguishable from similar species, although other species yet to be recorded in MontCo.
fm.Similar Species: Northern, Hagen, Tule and Boreal Bluet
fm.Habitat: heavily vegetated still water bodies, occasionally streams and rivers.

desc: Narrow bodied bluet with large post ocular spot/patch. Typically vividly coloreed, middle of abdomen mostly black.

fm.Male: Eyes blue with some black, narrow humeral stripe, abdomen mostly black with some blue on S1 and S7-10.
fm.Female: Eyes black and brown with yellowish post ocular spot. Blue thorax and large blue spot on S7.

desc: Largest Damselfly and tendency to hold wings outstretched when perched makes this one easy to identify.

fm.Male: Eyes light blue, thorax dark with two broad yellow stripes.  Some pruinesences on abdomen tip.
fm.Female: Eyes brown, thorax similar to male, abdomen much wider.
fm.Habitat: Slow streams with wooded banks or degraded semi-urban areas.

fm.Female: Eyes green and black,thorax gray with broad black median,abdomen blackish gray.
fm.Similar Species: Female can be confused with Eastern and Fragile Forktail
fm.Habitat: Still water and vernal pools, with vegetation.

fm.Male: Eyes blue and green, thorax metalic green above yellowish below, abdomen metalic green.  Some pruinecense.
fm.Female: Eyes green and yellow, metalic green thorax and abdomen including S9 and S10.
fm.Habitat: Lakes and ponds with abundant vegetation.

desc: Striking red abdomen contrasting with darker thorax distinctive.

fm.Male: Eyes red-brown, thorax black with red sides, abdomen bright red.
fm.Female: Eyes brown, thorax orangish, abdomen red-orange
fm.Habitat: Marshes with sedges and acid bogs

desc: Deeply blue bluet with multiple throacic stripes. Humeral strip divided by narrow blue stripe giving this species it's name. Female also has it.

fm.Male: Eyes mostly blue, blue with broad black humeral and median stripe each bisected with narrow blue stripe, abdomen very blue with black triangles on S6&7.
fm.Female: Similar to male but tan-brown and black.
fm.Similar Species: Other bluets may confuse at a distance but note all blue eyes and double thoracic striping.

desc: Combination of very blue head & thorax with mostly dark abdomen makes this a distinctive bluet. Very narrow humeral stripe, big blue ocular patches and narrow blue rings at base of abdominal segments key ID points.

fm.Male: Eyes blue, large ocular spot is blue, thorax very blue with narrow black humeral stripe and broader median stripe, abdomen mostly dark above begining on S2-7.
fm.Female: Similar to male but with wider humeral and thicker abdomen.
fm.Similar Species: Similar to Stream Bluet but note difference in S8 and blue of eyes and ocular patches.
fm.Habitat: Ponds and lakes with lots of vegetation.
